name: family_cohesion_quality
description: Measures the perceived quality of interpersonal relationships and sense of unity within the family, capturing both co-resident and non-resident family bonds and the factors respondents believe sustain or fracture them.

name: ideal_family_normativity
description: Captures respondents' normative beliefs about what constitutes an ideal family structure, what prevents its achievement in Mexico, and what actions could bring it about — reflecting underlying traditionalism versus pluralism in family ideology.

name: intergenerational_obligations
description: Measures beliefs about reciprocal duties between parents and children, including how long children should remain in the parental home and who bears responsibility for elder care, reflecting attitudes toward familial solidarity across generations.

name: conjugal_union_attitudes
description: Measures attitudes and behaviors related to forming, maintaining, and dissolving conjugal unions, including motivations for partnering, tolerance of infidelity, and perceived causes of divorce — reflecting normative orientations toward marriage and partnership stability.

name: family_value_transmission
description: Captures the values respondents consider most important within the family, what they learned from their family of origin and practice daily, and what fundamental values parents should pass on to children — reflecting the perceived role of the family as a moral socialization institution.
